Summer School

2025-2026 SUMMER SCHOOL ANNOUNCEMENT

1. The course registrations for the 2025-2026 Summer School will begin on Thursday, June 25, 2026, at 09:00 AM via SPARKS and will end on Friday, June 26, 2026, at 05:00 PM. Summer School courses will start on Monday, July 6, 2026.

2. Registration renewal for Preparatory Program students via SPARKS will begin on Monday, June 8, 2026, at 09:00 and end on Wednesday, June 10, 2026, at 17:00. Preparatory Program summer school classes will begin on Monday, June 15, 2026.

3.

Except for the online courses coded EL, if offered, all other courses will be conducted

face-to-face.

4. Students must first obtain approval from their advisors for the courses they select and then pay the required fee in advance. Summer school tuition fees are as follows: for undergraduate students,

25,000 TL for 3 or more KHAS credits

and

20,000 TL for 2 or fewer KHAS credits

(VAT included)

; for international students (including Turkish students enrolled under foreign student status),

670 USD for 3 or more KHAS credits

and

525 USD for 2 or fewer KHAS credits

(VAT included)

.

5. Summer School is free for students who start the Preparatory Program at TRACK-INTRO and TRACK-1 levels in the fall semester. The Preparatory Program Summer School fee is

75,000 TL including VAT

and for

international students

(including Turkish students registered as foreign students) it is

2,000 USD including VAT

. Course registration will be completed once the payment is approved by the Financial Control Directorate.

6. Students may take a maximum of

3 (three)

courses, not exceeding 12 KHAS credits or 21 ECTS credits in total, during the 2025-2026 Summer School. Students who are eligible to graduate at the end of the Summer School may be allowed to take

1 (one)

additional course with the approval of the faculty board. Students in this category may also benefit from the right to take a single-course exam in accordance with the relevant legislation.

7. Students who benefit from the University Entrance (ÖSYM) Success Scholarship (full scholarship) and the White Doves Scholarship, as well as students with a 100% internal horizontal transfer discount, do not pay any fees if they take the courses opened in the Summer School for the first time. Other scholarships and discounts do not apply during the Summer School. The full Summer School fee determined by the Board of Trustees will be charged.

8. A minimum of 10 students must register for a course to be offered. Courses with fewer than 10 students can remain open with the approval of the Vice Rector responsible for Education if the following conditions are met:

One of the students registered for the course will be eligible for graduation with the completion of that course.

The course has no scheduled class hours.

The course is conducted parallel to another course (at the same time and in the same classroom), and the total number of students in the parallel courses exceeds 10.

Courses that do not reach the required number of registered students will be announced to the respective students. Students whose registered courses are not offered will be refunded the fee for the unopened courses. Students who wish to take new courses and those who have not registered previously can add courses

during the course add-drop period

. The courses to be offered will be finalized based on registration status on Tuesday, July 7, 2026. Course additions can be made until Friday, July 10, 2026, but course drops and withdrawals are not permitted.

9. The conditions for taking courses from other universities during the Summer School are as follows:

The course must not be offered in the Summer School at our University.

The language of instruction for the course must be the same as that of the equivalent course at KHAS.

The course content must match the equivalent course at KHAS.

Courses from distance education or open education programs cannot be taken.

Graduation projects, term projects, internships, and similar courses, as well as one-on-one courses conducted under the supervision of an instructor without adhering to a specific curriculum, cannot be taken.

A student can take a maximum of 3 (three) courses from other universities with a maximum of 12 KHAS credits or 21 ECTS credits equivalent to Kadir Has University, excluding courses processed as FF, during his/her education.

If elective courses in the course plans are not offered in the Summer School and the student has failed these courses, they may take a course offered by another university with the approval of the relevant unit as an elective course.

These conditions must be determined by the relevant Faculty Board before taking the course. Therefore, students are expected to apply to the relevant Faculty Dean’s Office for approval before taking a course from another university.

Date

Details

June 25-26, 2026 (Thursday-Friday)

1. Select the courses you want to take on SPARKS and send them for advisor approval.

2. After the approval of your advisor, apply to the Financial Control Directorate for payment.

July 8-10, 2026 (Wednesday-Friday)

3. You can add a course or choose a course if you have not registered before.

4. If not all of the courses you registered for are offered, you can get a refund for the fees you paid for the unavailable courses from the Financial Control Directorate or add another course instead.

Preparatory Program

June 8-10, 2026 (Monday-Wednesday)

5. Renew your registration between June 8-10, 2026.

Summer School Preparatory Program classes will begin on Monday, June 15, 2026.

Summer School courses will begin on Monday, July 6, 2026.
